macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin falls below $77,000 as ETF outflows and liquidations intensify
items.summary: Bitcoin slid into the $76,000 area as spot ETF outflows and weaker market sentiment deepened a broad crypto selloff. Coinglass data showed more than 124,000 traders liquidated in 24 hours, with spot Bitcoin ETFs posting $290.4 million in net outflows and spot Ethereum ETFs seeing $65.7 million in outflows.

items.title: Cryptocurrency market weakens across major tokens, with Bitcoin Cash among biggest losers
items.summary: Broader crypto markets fell alongside Bitcoin, with Ethereum, XRP, and Dogecoin also posting notable declines. Market coverage highlighted Bitcoin Cash as one of the day’s sharpest losers, dropping more than 12% amid heavy selling pressure.

items.title: Crypto markets face heavy selling pressure amid renewed fear and leverage unwinds
items.summary: Market commentary pointed to a sharp risk-off move across crypto, driven by macro uncertainty, ETF outflows, and cascading liquidations. Analysts said Bitcoin’s recent breakdown has intensified attention on lower support levels and potential further downside.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Nintendo announces Pictonico, a mobile game that turns photos into mini-games
items.summary: Nintendo unveiled Pictonico, a new mobile game that uses users’ photos as the basis for mini-games. The company says the app is scheduled to launch later this month.

items.title: GTA 6 launch plans surface in new industry chatter
items.summary: A VGC news roundup notes renewed discussion around Grand Theft Auto 6’s release timing, with the game now said to be slated for November. The update follows earlier reporting that it had been expected in spring 2025.

items.title: Rocksteady credited on new Lego Batman project
items.summary: Rocksteady, best known for the Batman: Arkham series, has been listed as a co-developer on the upcoming Lego Batman game. The credit suggests the studio may be contributing despite its last release being Suicide Squad: Kill the Justice League.

items.title: Ghost of Yotei: Legends gets its final major update
items.summary: Sucker Punch has confirmed that Ghost of Yotei: Legends has received its last major content update. The multiplayer mode will remain playable, but no significant new content is planned.

items.title: Subnautica 2 continues huge early-access launch momentum
items.summary: VGC reports that Subnautica 2 sold 2 million copies in 12 hours and peaked at over 600,000 concurrent players. The game has also already received an early-access roadmap outlining upcoming additions.

items.title: Amazon reportedly cancels its Lord of the Rings MMO
items.summary: According to a gaming news roundup, Amazon has reportedly cancelled its Lord of the Rings MMO amid ongoing layoffs and broader pressure on its games division. The project had been in development for some time.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: OpenAI jury rules against Elon Musk in high-profile case
items.summary: A jury has ruled against Elon Musk after a three-week trial that concluded in less than two hours of deliberation. The decision is one of the biggest recent tech-industry legal developments.

items.title: Google’s redesigned app icons are rolling out
items.summary: Google has begun rolling out updated app icons that are more visually distinct than the versions introduced five years ago. The change is part of a broader refresh to the company’s product branding.

items.title: Sony raises PS Plus prices for one- and three-month plans
items.summary: Sony is increasing PlayStation Plus pricing for shorter subscription terms, citing ongoing market conditions. The change affects one-month and three-month plans.

items.title: Microsoft drops Teams ‘Together’ mode
items.summary: Microsoft is discontinuing the Teams feature that placed attendees in the same virtual room, saying it added too much implementation complexity. Users will be directed to the standard gallery view instead.

items.title: Google I/O 2026 keynote set to focus on AI and Android XR
items.summary: Google’s keynote is expected to include a large slate of AI announcements and possible Android XR updates. The event is one of the most important product and platform showcases in the tech calendar.
